message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

I think ThumbnailPanel is too complex. And a lot of the complexity stems from it trying to serve for both Merge mode and Split mode. I now realise, that the Modes have quite different requirements for the
  panel. So I suggest to not reuse ThumbnailPanel and implement it separately for each mode. Would it be feasable to have only one panel / scroll area for Merge mode? Than all the synchronisation and aritifical top/bottom offset wouldn't be necessary.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1 import { useRef, useState, useEffect, useCallback, forwardRef, useImperativeHandle } from 'react' 2 import { useVirtualizer } from '@tanstack/react-virtual' 3 import { Loader } from '@mantine/core' 4 import { usePageLoader } from '../hooks/usePageLoader' 5 6 const MIN_WIDTH = 120 7 const MAX_WIDTH = 480 8 export const DEFAULT_WIDTH = 220 9 export const DRAG_HANDLE_WIDTH = 4 10 export const ITEM_PADDING = 8 11 export const ITEM_GAP = 0 12 export const LABEL_HEIGHT = 20 13 // DIN A4 portrait aspect ratio (210 × 297 mm) 14 export const PAGE_ASPECT = 297 / 210 15 16 const DEFAULT_THUMB_HEIGHT = Math.round((DEFAULT_WIDTH - ITEM_PADDING * 2) * PAGE_ASPECT) 17 export const HALF_THUMB_HEIGHT = Math.round(DEFAULT_THUMB_HEIGHT / 2) 18 19 const STRIP_LABEL_HEIGHT = 28 20 21 export interface ThumbnailPanelHandle { 22 scrollTo: (top: number) => void 23 } 24 25 interface Props { 26 pdfPath: string 27 pageCount: number 28 selectedPage: number // 1-indexed 29 onSelectPage: (page: number) => void 30 label?: string 31 width?: number 32 hideDragHandle?: boolean 33 onWidthChange?: (width: number) => void 34 onScroll?: (scrollTop: number) => void 35 hideScrollbar?: boolean 36 topPadding?: number 37 bottomPadding?: number 38 pageNumberLabel?: (index: number) => number 39 } 40 41 const ThumbnailPanel = forwardRef(function ThumbnailPanel( 42 { pdfPath, pageCount, selectedPage, onSelectPage, label, width: controlledWidth, hideDragHandle, onWidthChange, onScroll, hideScrollbar, topPadding = 0, bottomPadding = 0, pageNumberLabel }, 43 ref, 44 ) { 45 const [internalWidth, setInternalWidth] = useState(DEFAULT_WIDTH) 46 const panelWidth = controlledWidth ?? internalWidth 47 48 const thumbWidth = panelWidth - ITEM_PADDING * 2 49 const thumbHeight = Math.round(thumbWidth * PAGE_ASPECT) 50 const itemHeight = thumbHeight + LABEL_HEIGHT + ITEM_PADDING + ITEM_GAP 51 52 const scrollRef = useRef(null) 53 54 const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({ 55 count: pageCount, 56 getScrollElement: () => scrollRef.current, 57 estimateSize: () => itemHeight, 58 overscan: 3, 59 paddingStart: topPadding, 60 }) 61 62 // Re-estimate row heights when panel is resized 63 useEffect(() => { 64 virtualizer.measure() 65 // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps 66 }, [itemHeight]) 67 68 const virtualItems = virtualizer.getVirtualItems() 69 const { getSrc, isLoading, load, invalidate } = usePageLoader(pdfPath, thumbWidth) 70 71 useEffect(() => { 72 for (const item of virtualItems) load(item.index + 1) 73 }) 74 75 // Scroll selected page into view (e.g. after keyboard navigation) 76 useEffect(() => { 77 virtualizer.scrollToIndex(selectedPage - 1) 89 } else if (e.key === 'ArrowRight' && selectedPage < pageCount) { 90 e.preventDefault() 91 onSelectPage(selectedPage + 1) 92 } 93 }, [selectedPage, pageCount, onSelectPage]) 94 95 const startDrag = (e: React.MouseEvent) => { 96 const startX = e.clientX 97 const startWidth = panelWidth 98 const clamp = (w: number) => Math.max(MIN_WIDTH, Math.min(MAX_WIDTH, w)) 99 100 const onMove = (ev: MouseEvent) => { 101 const w = clamp(startWidth + ev.clientX - 1	import { useRef, useState } from 'react'
2	import { Box, Button, Center, Group, SegmentedControl, Text } from '@mantine/core'
3	import { notifications } from '@mantine/notifications'
4	import { MergePDFs, OpenPDF, PageCount, SavePDF } from '../../wailsjs/go/main/App'
5	import ThumbnailPanel, {
6	  DEFAULT_WIDTH, DRAG_HANDLE_WIDTH, HALF_THUMB_HEIGHT,
7	  ITEM_PADDING, ITEM_GAP, LABEL_HEIGHT, PAGE_ASPECT,
8	  ThumbnailPanelHandle,
9	} from './ThumbnailPanel'
10	
11	// Maps a panel's 0-based page index to its output PDF page number.
12	// isFirst: true if this panel's pages go first in the interleave (odd output pages).
13	// countOther: page count of the other panel.
14	function makePageNumberLabel(isFirst: boolean, countOther: number) {
15	  const minCount = countOther  // interleaved section length = min(count, countOther), but we only know countOther here
16	  return (index: number) => {
17	    const n = index + 1  // 1-based
18	    if (n <= minCount) {
19	      return isFirst ? 2 * n - 1 : 2 * n
20	    }
21	    // extra pages appended after interleave
22	    return 2 * minCount + (n - minCount)
23	  }
24	}
25	
26	function itemHeight(panelWidth: number) {
27	  const thumbWidth = panelWidth - ITEM_PADDING * 2
28	  const thumbHeight = Math.round(thumbWidth * PAGE_ASPECT)
29	  return thumbHeight + LABEL_HEIGHT + ITEM_PADDING + ITEM_GAP
30	}
31	
32	function basename(p: string) {
33	  return p.split(/[\\/]/).pop() ?? p
34	}
35	
36	type FirstPageIn = 'a' | 'b'
37	
38	export default function MergeMode() {
39	  const [pathA, setPathA] = useState<string | null>(null)
40	  const [countA, setCountA] = useState(0)
41	  const [pathB, setPathB] = useState<string | null>(null)
42	  const [countB, setCountB] = useState(0)
43	  const [pageA, setPageA] = useState(1)
44	  const [pageB, setPageB] = useState(1)
45	  const [firstPageIn, setFirstPageIn] = useState<FirstPageIn>('a')
46	  const [sharedWidth, setSharedWidth] = useState(DEFAULT_WIDTH)
47	  const [merging, setMerging] = useState(false)
48	  const refA = useRef<ThumbnailPanelHandle>(null)
49	  const refB = useRef<ThumbnailPanelHandle>(null)
50	  const syncing = useRef(false)
51	
52	  const handleScrollA = (top: number) => {
53	    if (syncing.current) return
54	    syncing.current = true
55	    refB.current?.scrollTo(top)
56	    syncing.current = false
57	  }
58	
59	  const handleScrollB = (top: number) => {
60	    if (syncing.current) return
61	    syncing.current = true
62	    refA.current?.scrollTo(top)
63	    syncing.current = false
64	  }
65	
66	  const handleChooseA = async () => {
67	    const p = await OpenPDF()
68	    if (!p) return
69	    const count = await PageCount(p)
70	    setPathA(p)
71	    setCountA(count)
72	    setPageA(1)
73	  }
74	
75	  const handleChooseB = async () => {
76	    const p = await OpenPDF()
77	    if (!p) return
78	    const count = await PageCount(p)
79	    setPathB(p)
80	    setCountB(count)
81	    setPageB(1)
82	  }
83	
84	  const handleMerge = async () => {
85	    if (!pathA || !pathB) return
86	    const outPath = await SavePDF()
87	    if (!outPath) return
88	    setMerging(true)
89	    try {
90	      const effectiveFirst = firstPageIn === 'a' ? pathA : pathB
91	      const effectiveSecond = firstPageIn === 'a' ? pathB : pathA
92	      await MergePDFs(effectiveFirst, effectiveSecond, outPath, false)
93	      notifications.show({ message: `Saved to ${outPath}`, color: 'green' })
94	    } catch (e) {
95	      notifications.show({ title: 'Merge failed', message: String(e), color: 'red' })
96	    } finally {
97	      setMerging(false)
98	    }
99	  }
100	
101	  const bothLoaded = pathA !== null && pathB !== null
102	
103	  // Offsets only apply once both files are loaded (they describe interleave order).
104	  const topPaddingA = bothLoaded && firstPageIn === 'b' ? HALF_THUMB_HEIGHT : 0
105	  const topPaddingB = bothLoaded && firstPageIn === 'a' ? HALF_THUMB_HEIGHT : 0
106	
107	  // Equalise the maximum scrollTop of both strips.
108	  // maxScroll_first  = countFirst  * ihFirst  + 0             - H
109	  // maxScroll_second = countSecond * ihSecond + HALF_THUMB_HEIGHT - H
110	  // diff = maxScroll_second - maxScroll_first
111	  // bottomPadding_first  = max(0,  diff)
112	  // bottomPadding_second = max(0, -diff)
113	  const ih = itemHeight(sharedWidth)
114	  const [countFirst, ihFirst, countSecond, ihSecond] = firstPageIn === 'a'
115	    ? [countA, ih, countB, ih]
116	    : [countB, ih, countA, ih]
117	  const diff = countSecond * ihSecond - countFirst * ihFirst + HALF_THUMB_HEIGHT
118	  const bottomPaddingFirst  = Math.max(0,  diff)
119	  const bottomPaddingSecond = Math.max(0, -diff)
120	  const bottomPaddingA = firstPageIn === 'a' ? bottomPaddingFirst : bottomPaddingSecond
121	  const bottomPaddingB = firstPageIn === 'a' ? bottomPaddingSecond : bottomPaddingFirst
122	
123	  const aIsFirst = firstPageIn === 'a'
124	  const pageLabelA = bothLoaded ? makePageNumberLabel(aIsFirst,  aIsFirst ? countB : countA) : undefined
125	  const pageLabelB = bothLoaded ? makePageNumberLabel(!aIsFirst, aIsFirst ? countA : countB) : undefined
126	
127	  // A has no drag handle; B has one — toolbar columns match accordingly
128	  const colWidthA = sharedWidth
129	  const colWidthB = sharedWidth + DRAG_HANDLE_WIDTH
130	
131	  return (
132	    <Box style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', height: '100%' }}>
133	      {/* Toolbar: columns A and B aligned with their strips, then controls on the right */}
134	      <Box
135	        style={{
136	          flexShrink: 0,
137	          borderBottom: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)',
138	          display: 'flex',
139	          alignItems: 'stretch',
140	        }}
141	      >
142	        <FilePickerColumn
143	          label="File A"
144	          path={pathA}
145	          width={colWidthA}
146	          onChoose={handleChooseA}
147	        />
148	        <FilePickerColumn
149	          label="File B"
150	          path={pathB}
151	          width={colWidthB}
152	          onChoose={handleChooseB}
153	        />
154	        <Group gap={12} px={12} style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'flex-end' }}>
155	          <Group gap={8}>
156	            <Text size="sm" c="dimmed">First page in</Text>
157	            <SegmentedControl
158	              size="xs"
159	              value={firstPageIn}
160	              onChange={(v) => setFirstPageIn(v as FirstPageIn)}
161	              data={[
162	                { label: 'File A', value: 'a' },
163	                { label: 'File B', value: 'b' },
164	              ]}
165	            />
166	          </Group>
167	          <Button size="sm" disabled={!bothLoaded} loading={merging} onClick={handleMerge}>
168	            Merge & Save
169	          </Button>
170	        </Group>
171	      </Box>
172	
173	      <Box style={{ flex: 1, overflow: 'hidden', display: 'flex' }}>
174	        {pathA && (
175	          <ThumbnailPanel
176	            ref={refA}
177	            pdfPath={pathA}
178	            pageCount={countA}
179	            selectedPage={pageA}
180	            onSelectPage={setPageA}
181	            width={sharedWidth}
182	            hideDragHandle
183	            onScroll={handleScrollA}
184	            hideScrollbar
185	            topPadding={topPaddingA}
186	            bottomPadding={bottomPaddingA}
187	            pageNumberLabel={pageLabelA}
188	          />
189	        )}
190	        {pathB && (
191	          <ThumbnailPanel
192	            ref={refB}
193	            pdfPath={pathB}
194	            pageCount={countB}
195	            selectedPage={pageB}
196	            onSelectPage={setPageB}
197	            width={sharedWidth}
198	            onWidthChange={setSharedWidth}
199	            onScroll={handleScrollB}
200	            topPadding={topPaddingB}
201	            bottomPadding={bottomPaddingB}
202	            pageNumberLabel={pageLabelB}
203	          />
204	        )}
205	        {!pathA && !pathB && (
206	          <Center style={{ flex: 1 }}>
207	            <Text c="dimmed" size="sm">Choose PDF files above to begin</Text>
208	          </Center>
209	        )}
210	      </Box>
211	    </Box>
212	  )
213	}
214	
215	
216	function FilePickerColumn({
217	  label,
218	  path,
219	  width,
220	  onChoose,
221	}: {
222	  label: string
223	  path: string | null
224	  width: number
225	  onChoose: () => void
226	}) {
227	  return (
228	    <Box
229	      style={{
230	        width,
231	        flexShrink: 0,
232	        padding: '6px 8px',
233	        borderRight: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)',
234	        display: 'flex',
235	        flexDirection: 'column',
236	        justifyContent: 'center',
237	        gap: 2,
238	      }}
239	    >
240	      <Group justify="space-between" gap={4} wrap="nowrap">
241	        <Text size="xs" c="dimmed">{label}</Text>
242	        <Button size="xs" variant="default" onClick={onChoose}>Choose…</Button>
243	      </Group>
244	      <Text
245	        size="xs"
246	        style={{ overflow: 'hidden', textOverflow: 'ellipsis', whiteSpace: 'nowrap' }}
247	        title={path ?? undefined}
248	      >
249	        {path ? basename(path) : '—'}
250	      </Text>
251	    </Box>
252	  )
253	}
254

lastPrompt: I think ThumbnailPanel is too complex. And a lot of the complexity stems from it trying to serve for both Merge mode and Split mode. I now realise, that the Modes have quite different requirements for…

aiTitle: Refactor ThumbnailPanel for separate mode implementations

content.text:
Yes, a single scroll area for Merge mode is very feasible and would eliminate all that complexity. Here's the idea: **Single virtualizer, rows of pairs.** Instead of two synced panels, one virtualizer renders `max(countA, countB)` rows. Each row has A's thumbnail on the left and B's thumbnail on the right, side by side. Scrolling is one action, naturally synchronized. The "first page in" toggle no longer needs an offset — it just determines which column's pages get odd output numbers and which get even. The bottom-padding equalization math disappears entirely. No refs, no `syncing` flag, no `handleScrollA/B`. The tricky parts are minor: - When counts differ, the shorter side renders blank/empty cells for trailing rows - The resize drag handle could live between the two columns (a single handle controlling both widths simultaneously, or each column independently) - Selection highlight still tracks `pageA` and `pageB` independently, but they're rendered in the same item loop **Rough structure:** ```
// single scroll container {virtualItems.map(item => (
// or blank
))}
``` This is cleaner, more intuitive, and the visual representation of "these two pages interleave" is more obvious to users too — you see them next to each other rather than offset. I'd say go for it: split `ThumbnailPanel` into a simpler `SplitModePanel` (essentially what it is today minus merge-specific props) and a new `MergeModeStrip` that's purpose-built for paired rows.

message.content:
Okay. Here's the plan:
Build a new MergeModeThumbnailPanel in a new file and render it next to the existing one, so I can compare visually. The new panel should look and behave pretty much like the combination of the existing 2 panels:
- one scroll area containing both columns
- column containing the second page should be offset by halve a page height
- right vertical border of the panel should allow to resize the whole panel; both columns adapt there width simultaniously
- numbering of pages should remain as is: the numbers should match the pages as they appear in the output PDF
- top row with labels, buttons and filenames as is.

content.content:
1	import { useState } from 'react'
2	import { Box, Button, Center } from '@mantine/core'
3	import ThumbnailPanel from './ThumbnailPanel'
4	import DetailPanel from './DetailPanel'
5	import { OpenPDF, PageCount } from '../../wailsjs/go/main/App'
6	
7	export default function SplitMode() {
8	  const [pdfPath, setPdfPath] = useState<string | null>(null)
9	  const [pageCount, setPageCount] = useState(0)
10	  const [selectedPage, setSelectedPage] = useState(1)
11	
12	  const handleOpen = async () => {
13	    const path = await OpenPDF()
14	    if (!path) return
15	    const count = await PageCount(path)
16	    setPdfPath(path)
17	    setPageCount(count)
18	    setSelectedPage(1)
19	  }
20	
21	  return (
22	    <Box style={{ display: 'flex', height: '100%' }}>
23	      {pdfPath ? (
24	        <ThumbnailPanel
25	          pdfPath={pdfPath}
26	          pageCount={pageCount}
27	          selectedPage={selectedPage}
28	          onSelectPage={setSelectedPage}
29	        />
30	      ) : null}
31	
32	      {pdfPath ? (
33	        <DetailPanel
34	          pdfPath={pdfPath}
35	          pageNum={selectedPage}
36	          pageCount={pageCount}
37	          onNavigate={setSelectedPage}
38	        />
39	      ) : (
40	        <Box style={{ flex: 1, display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center' }}>
41	          <Center>
42	            <Button onClick={handleOpen}>Open PDF</Button>
43	          </Center>
44	        </Box>
45	      )}
46	    </Box>
47	  )
48	}
49

1	import { useState } from 'react'
2	import { Box, Group, Tabs, Text } from '@mantine/core'
3	import MergeMode from './components/MergeMode'
4	import SplitMode from './components/SplitMode'
5	
6	type AppMode = 'split' | 'merge'
7	
8	export default function App() {
9	  const [mode, setMode] = useState<AppMode>('split')
10	
11	  return (
12	    <Box style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', height: '100vh', overflow: 'hidden' }}>
13	      <Box
14	        component="header"
15	        style={{
16	          height: 48,
17	          flexShrink: 0,
18	          borderBottom: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)',
19	          display: 'flex',
20	          alignItems: 'center',
21	          paddingInline: 'var(--mantine-spacing-md)',
22	        }}
23	      >
24	        <Group justify="space-between" style={{ width: '100%' }}>
25	          <Text fw={600} size="sm">Paper Scan Processor</Text>
26	          <Tabs value={mode} onChange={(v) => v && setMode(v as AppMode)}>
27	            <Tabs.List>
28	              <Tabs.Tab value="split">Split</Tabs.Tab>
29	              <Tabs.Tab value="merge">Merge</Tabs.Tab>
30	            </Tabs.List>
31	          </Tabs>
32	        </Group>
33	      </Box>
34	      <Box style={{ flex: 1, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
35	        {mode === 'split' ? <SplitMode /> : <MergeMode />}
36	      </Box>
37	    </Box>
38	  )
39	}
40

file.content:
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Box, Group, Tabs, Text } from '@mantine/core'
import MergeMode from './components/MergeMode'
import SplitMode from './components/SplitMode'

type AppMode = 'split' | 'merge'

export default function App() {
  const [mode, setMode] = useState<AppMode>('split')

  return (
    <Box style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', height: '100vh', overflow: 'hidden' }}>
      <Box
        component="header"
        style={{
          height: 48,
          flexShrink: 0,
          borderBottom: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)',
          display: 'flex',
          alignItems: 'center',
          paddingInline: 'var(--mantine-spacing-md)',
        }}
      >
        <Group justify="space-between" style={{ width: '100%' }}>
          <Text fw={600} size="sm">Paper Scan Processor</Text>
          <Tabs value={mode} onChange={(v) => v && setMode(v as AppMode)}>
            <Tabs.List>
              <Tabs.Tab value="split">Split</Tabs.Tab>
              <Tabs.Tab value="merge">Merge</Tabs.Tab>
            </Tabs.List>
          </Tabs>
        </Group>
      </Box>
      <Box style={{ flex: 1, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
        {mode === 'split' ? <SplitMode /> : <MergeMode />}
      </Box>
    </Box>
  )
}

content.text: Let me also check the page loader hook.

content.content:
1	import { useRef, useState, useEffect } from 'react'
2	import { RenderPage } from '../../wailsjs/go/main/App'
3	
4	interface PageLoader {
5	  getSrc: (page: number) => string | undefined
6	  isLoading: (page: number) => boolean
7	  isFailed: (page: number) => boolean
8	  load: (page: number) => void
9	  invalidate: () => void
10	}
11	
12	export function usePageLoader(pdfPath: string, widthPx: number): PageLoader {
13	  const cacheRef = useRef(new Map<number, string>())
14	  const loadingRef = useRef(new Set<number>())
15	  const failedRef = useRef(new Set<number>())
16	  const [, setTick] = useState(0)
17	
18	  const invalidate = () => {
19	    cacheRef.current.clear()
20	    loadingRef.current.clear()
21	    failedRef.current.clear()
22	    setTick(t => t + 1)
23	  }
24	
25	  useEffect(() => {
26	    invalidate()
27	  // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
28	  }, [pdfPath])
29	
30	  const load = (page: number) => {
31	    if (cacheRef.current.has(page) || loadingRef.current.has(page) || failedRef.current.has(page)) return
32	    loadingRef.current.add(page)
33	    RenderPage(pdfPath, page, widthPx)
34	      .then((b64: string) => {
35	        cacheRef.current.set(page, `data:image/png;base64,${b64}`)
36	        loadingRef.current.delete(page)
37	        setTick(t => t + 1)
38	      })
39	      .catch((err: unknown) => {
40	        console.error(`RenderPage failed for page ${page}:`, err)
41	        loadingRef.current.delete(page)
42	        failedRef.current.add(page)
43	        setTick(t => t + 1)
44	      })
45	  }
46	
47	  return {
48	    getSrc: (page) => cacheRef.current.get(page),
49	    isLoading: (page) => loadingRef.current.has(page),
50	    isFailed: (page) => failedRef.current.has(page),
51	    load,
52	    invalidate,
53	  }
54	}
55

content.text:
Clean build. The new file is at `frontend/src/components/MergeModeThumbnailPanel.tsx`. You can run the app now to compare them side by side — the red bar separates the old pair of panels (left) from the new single panel (right).

A few notes on the new panel's behavior vs the old:
- **Offset** — uses the actual current thumbnail height ÷ 2 rather than the constant based on default width, so it scales correctly when you resize
- **Scroll** — one scroll bar drives both columns; clicking a page in either column scrolls the whole panel to show it
- **Resize** — the drag handle on the far right adjusts total width; both columns stay equal halves
- **Page numbers** — same `makePageNumberLabel` logic, duplicated locally for now

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content: Built MergeModeThumbnailPanel, a single-scroll replacement for the two synced ThumbnailPanels in Merge mode. Run the app and compare the new panel (right of the red separator) against the old one.

input.command:
cd /Users/ben/git/paper-scan-processor && git add frontend/src/components/MergeModeThumbnailPanel.tsx frontend/src/components/MergeMode.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add MergeModeThumbnailPanel for side-by-side visual comparison

New component uses a single scroll area with two virtualizers sharing
one scroll element, replacing the two-panel sync approach. Rendered
next to the existing panels (separated by a red bar) for comparison.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit new component and MergeMode changes
